generatedSentence: The graying grandpa, a retired collector, sat at his desk, his wallet surprisingly light despite years of frugality and consistent earnings. He often browsed a vintage magazine, remembering a simpler time, feeling the hackneyed routines of his household create small hitches in his peace. There was an anterior ache, an appendix of regret, that pressed on him. He felt like he was juggling responsibilities, yearning for an arrangement that truly affects positive change, helping a new blossom of hope, a known path to a better appearance. His job felt like a mere occasion, but he sought inner harmony. An insidious infection had locked down the ancient monolith database, threatening his place on the leaderboard of forgotten heroes. His old valise contained a single shuriken, a reminder of his past. A cracking message on his screen showed the impact of the data breach, leaving digital bruises on the global network. Visitors to the upland archives were pinned down, their files corrupted. He saw images of a giant, menacing mayonnaise-colored construct, an eyesore to progress, designed to juvenilize anyone who opposed it. He pulled on his trousers, determined to reveal its transparency, an immortalizing act, a true guru move, frankly an immense risk, making him a sucker for justice. He strapped his swords onto his back, ready to chase the star of his quest. A rogue drake agent, rumored to be a superstar of the dark web, revved his engine, "Vroom!" as he tried to evade the oracles he’d scorned. The protagonist knew he was overpacking for a mission into a nebula of data, where even negligible tickets could prove vital. He plunged into the welter of the aquatic server farms, navigating a magical, volumetric payload of information. He found the rogue agent trying to steal a rare olive-colored data shard, amidst tatters of old schematics, a relic of chastity kept for centuries. He had to adjourn this theft before the agent could jetset away, a truly capricious champion who rejects all authority, like a sleek jaguar, ideally outrunning justice. The scent of saffron and a glass of enriched whisky signaled victory. The protagonist felt amped, his heart finally in harmony after the intense struggle. He’d even learned some breakdancing moves from a fellow actor who was a locksmith for digital systems, securing the headquarters from future threats. As he walked along the moonlit walkway, he remembered his grandpa’s wisdom. The gear for his next adventure was ready. He felt a profound sense of rejuvenates in his spirit. He leaned in for a kiss with his partner, looking forward to the outcomes of their daring longboard ride into the future, saying "anyways, that was a bleak experience, but not entirely gross." The old system, though buckled, was now safe.
panelSentences: The graying grandpa, a retired collector, sat at his desk, his wallet surprisingly light despite years of frugality and consistent earnings. He often browsed a vintage magazine, remembering a simpler time, feeling the hackneyed routines of his household create small hitches in his peace. There was an anterior ache, an appendix of regret, that pressed on him. He felt like he was juggling responsibilities, yearning for an arrangement that truly affects positive change, helping a new blossom of hope, a known path to a better appearance. His job felt like a mere occasion, but he sought inner harmony.

An insidious infection had locked down the ancient monolith database, threatening his place on the leaderboard of forgotten heroes. His old valise contained a single shuriken, a reminder of his past. A cracking message on his screen showed the impact of the data breach, leaving digital bruises on the global network. Visitors to the upland archives were pinned down, their files corrupted. He saw images of a giant, menacing mayonnaise-colored construct, an eyesore to progress, designed to juvenilize anyone who opposed it. He pulled on his trousers, determined to reveal its transparency, an immortalizing act, a true guru move, frankly an immense risk, making him a sucker for justice.

He strapped his swords onto his back, ready to chase the star of his quest. A rogue drake agent, rumored to be a superstar of the dark web, revved his engine, "Vroom!" as he tried to evade the oracles he’d scorned. The protagonist knew he was overpacking for a mission into a nebula of data, where even negligible tickets could prove vital. He plunged into the welter of the aquatic server farms, navigating a magical, volumetric payload of information. He found the rogue agent trying to steal a rare olive-colored data shard, amidst tatters of old schematics, a relic of chastity kept for centuries. He had to adjourn this theft before the agent could jetset away, a truly capricious champion who rejects all authority, like a sleek jaguar, ideally outrunning justice.

The scent of saffron and a glass of enriched whisky signaled victory. The protagonist felt amped, his heart finally in harmony after the intense struggle. He’d even learned some breakdancing moves from a fellow actor who was a locksmith for digital systems, securing the headquarters from future threats. As he walked along the moonlit walkway, he remembered his grandpa’s wisdom. The gear for his next adventure was ready. He felt a profound sense of rejuvenates in his spirit. He leaned in for a kiss with his partner, looking forward to the outcomes of their daring longboard ride into the future, saying "anyways, that was a bleak experience, but not entirely gross." The old system, though buckled, was now safe.
rawPanelSentences: The **gray**ing **grandpa**, a retired **collector**, sat at his **desk**, his **wallet** surprisingly light despite years of **frugality** and consistent **earnings**. He often browsed a vintage **magazine**, remembering a simpler time, feeling the **hackneyed** routines of his **household** create small **hitches** in his peace. There was an **anterior** ache, an **appendix** of regret, that **pressed** on him. He felt like he was **juggling** responsibilities, yearning for an **arrangement** that truly **affects** positive change, helping a new **blossom** of hope, a known path to a better **appearance**. His **job** felt like a mere **occasion**, but he sought inner **harmony**.

An **insidious infection** had **locked** down the ancient **monolith** database, threatening his place on the **leaderboard** of forgotten heroes. His old **valise** contained a single **shuriken**, a reminder of his past. A **cracking** message on his **screen** showed the **impact** of the data breach, leaving digital **bruises** on the global network. **Visitors** to the **upland** archives were **pinned** down, their files corrupted. He saw images of a giant, menacing **mayonnaise**-colored construct, an **eyesore** to progress, designed to **juvenilize** anyone who opposed it. He pulled on his **trousers**, determined to reveal its **transparency**, an **immortalizing** act, a true **guru** move, **frankly** an **immense** risk, making him a **sucker** for justice.

He strapped his **swords** onto his back, ready to chase the **star** of his quest. A rogue **drake** agent, rumored to be a **superstar** of the dark web, revved his engine, "Vroom!" as he tried to evade the **oracles** he’d scorned. The protagonist knew he was **overpacking** for a mission into a **nebula** of data, where even **negligible tickets** could prove vital. He plunged into the **welter** of the **aquatic** server farms, navigating a **magical**, **volumetric payload** of information. He found the rogue agent trying to steal a rare **olive**-colored data shard, amidst **tatters** of old schematics, a relic of **chastity** kept for **centuries**. He had to **adjourn** this theft before the agent could **jetset** away, a truly **capricious champion** who **rejects** all authority, like a sleek **jaguar**, **ideally** outrunning justice.

The scent of **saffron** and a glass of **enriched whisky** signaled victory. The protagonist felt **amped**, his heart finally in **harmony** after the intense struggle. He’d even learned some **breakdancing** moves from a fellow **actor** who was a **locksmith** for digital systems, securing the **headquarters** from future threats. As he walked along the moonlit **walkway**, he remembered his **grandpa**’s wisdom. The **gear** for his next adventure was ready. He felt a profound sense of **rejuvenates** in his spirit. He leaned in for a **kiss** with his partner, looking forward to the **outcomes** of their daring **longboard** ride into the future, saying "anyways, that was a **bleak** experience, but not entirely **gross**." The old system, though **buckled**, was now safe.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, gritty neo-noir comic style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A grizzled, middle-aged man with a neatly trimmed gray beard, wearing a worn leather jacket and dark jeans, and a woman with striking silver streaks in her dark, swept-back hair, wearing a sleek, tactical jumpsuit. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled man, his face etched with worry, examining a complex holographic map on a desk, a discarded whisky glass nearby. (Close-up, dramatic cinematic lighting, cel-shading, bold ink outlines) TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The woman, her brow furrowed, skillfully navigating a sleek, futuristic longboard through a dimly lit, narrow walkway, avoiding holographic security lasers. (Dynamic low-angle shot, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting) BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Both protagonists in a fierce struggle against a towering, metallic automaton in a cavernous, high-tech server room, the grizzled man attempting to hack a console while the woman fends off the drone with a shuriken. (Wide shot, intense cinematic lighting, cel-shading, bold ink outlines) BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The grizzled man and woman, both bearing signs of their struggle but smiling subtly, sharing a celebratory kiss on an opulent balcony overlooking a vibrant, future city at dawn. (Medium shot, warm cinematic lighting, cel-shading, bold ink outlines)

definitions.definition: An achromatic colour intermediate between black and white.
An animal or thing of grey colour, such as a horse, badger, or salmon.
An extraterrestrial humanoid with grayish skin, bulbous black eyes, and an enlarged head.
A penny with a tail on both sides, used for cheating.
To become gray.
definitions.example: My hair is beginning to gray.

definitions.definition: To cause to become gray.
To turn progressively older, alluding to graying of hair through aging (used in context of the population of a geographic region)
definitions.example: the graying of America

definitions.definition: To give a soft effect to (a photograph) by covering the negative while printing with a ground-glass plate.
Having a color somewhere between white and black, as the ash of an ember.
Dreary, gloomy.
Having an indistinct, disputed or uncertain quality.
Relating to older people.
definitions.example: the gray dollar, i.e. the purchasing power of the elderly

definitions.definition: A mother of someone's parent.
A female ancestor or progenitor.
A light meal.
An item of food eaten between meals.
A very sexy and attractive person.
To eat a light meal.
To eat between meals.
Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.
A luminous celestial body, made up of plasma (particularly hydrogen and helium) and having a spherical shape. Depending on context the sun may or may not be included.
A concave polygon with regular, pointy protrusions and indentations, generally with five or six points.
An actor in a leading role.
definitions.example: Many Hollywood stars attended the launch party.

definitions.definition: An exceptionally talented or famous person, often in a specific field; a celebrity.
definitions.example: His teacher tells us he is a star pupil.

definitions.definition: An asterisk (*).
A symbol used to rate hotels, films, etc. with a higher number of stars denoting better quality.

A simple dance, or part of a dance, where a group of four dancers each put their right or left hand in the middle and turn around in a circle. You call them right-hand stars or left-hand stars, depending on the hand which is in the middle.
A planet supposed to influence one's destiny.
definitions.example: What's in the stars for you today? Find out in our horoscope.

definitions.definition: A star-shaped ornament worn on the breast to indicate rank or honour.
A composition of combustible matter used in the heading of rockets, in mines, etc., which, exploding in the air, presents a starlike appearance.
To appear as a featured performer or headliner, especially in an entertainment program.
To feature (a performer or a headliner), especially in a movie or an entertainment program.
To mark with a star or asterisk.
To set or adorn with stars, or bright, radiating bodies; to bespangle.
To shine like a star.
A long-bladed weapon with a hilt, and usually a pommel and cross-guard, which is designed to stab, slash, and/or hack.
A suit in the minor arcana in tarot.
A card of this suit.
One of the end bars by which the lay of a hand loom is suspended.
The weapon, often used as a heraldic charge.
A large, single block of stone which is a natural feature; or a block of stone or other similar material used in architecture and sculpture, especially one carved into a monument in ancient times.
Anything massive, uniform, and unmovable, especially a towering and impersonal cultural, political, or social organization or structure.
A substrate having many tiny channels that is cast as a single piece, which is used as a stationary phase for chromatography, as a catalytic surface, etc.
A dead tree whose height and size have been reduced by breaking off or cutting its branches.
To create (something) as, or convert (one or more things) into, a monolith.
A board showing the ranking of leaders in a competition.
An advertisement on a web page spanning the width of the page and shallow in height.
To become fastened in place.
definitions.example: If you put the brakes on too hard, the wheels will lock.

definitions.definition: To fasten with a lock.
definitions.example: Remember to lock the door when you leave.

definitions.definition: To be capable of becoming fastened in place.
definitions.example: This door locks with a key.

definitions.definition: To intertwine or dovetail.
definitions.example: We locked arms and stepped out into the night.

definitions.definition: (break dancing) To freeze one's body or a part thereof in place.
definitions.example: a pop and lock routine

definitions.definition: To furnish (a canal) with locks.
To raise or lower (a boat) in a lock.
To seize (e.g. the sword arm of an antagonist) by turning the left arm around it, to disarm him.
To modify (a thread) so that users cannot make new posts in it.
(WMF jargon) To prevent a page from being edited by other users.
definitions.example: Frequently vandalized pages are generally locked to prevent further damage.

definitions.definition: Having undergone locking; secured by a lock.
definitions.example: A locked door barred the way.

definitions.definition: (Dublin) Very drunk.
(of a phone) Bound to a carrier.
Before in place.
Before or earlier in time; prior to; preceding.
Nearer the forward end; nearer the head of an animal or the front of a human.
Something attached to something else; an attachment or accompaniment.
A text added to the end of a book or an article, containing additional information.
The vermiform appendix, an inner organ that can become inflamed.
Any process, prolongation or projection.
To make uninteresting or trite by frequent use.
To use as a hackney.
To carry in a hackney coach.
Repeated too often.
definitions.example: The sermon was full of hackneyed phrases and platitudes.

definitions.definition: Let out for hire.
A sudden pull.
Any of various knots used to attach a rope to an object other than another rope.
A fastener or connection point, as for a trailer.
definitions.example: His truck sported a heavy-duty hitch for his boat.

definitions.definition: A problem, delay or source of difficulty.
definitions.example: The banquet went off without a hitch

definitions.definition: A hidden or unfavorable condition or element; a catch.
definitions.example: The deal sounds too good to be true. What's the hitch?

definitions.definition: A period of time spent in the military.
definitions.example: She served two hitches in Vietnam.

definitions.definition: To pull with a jerk.
definitions.example: She hitched her jeans up and then tightened her belt.

definitions.definition: To attach, tie or fasten.
definitions.example: He hitched the bedroll to his backpack and went camping.

definitions.definition: To marry oneself to; especially to get hitched.
Contraction of hitchhike, to thumb a ride.
definitions.example: to hitch a ride

definitions.definition: To become entangled or caught; to be linked or yoked; to unite; to cling.
To move interruptedly or with halts, jerks, or steps; said of something obstructed or impeded.
To strike the legs together in going, as horses; to interfere.
Collectively, all the persons who live in a given house; a family including attendants, servants etc.; a domestic or family establishment.
A line of ancestry; a race or house.
Belonging to the same house and family.
Found in or having its origin in a home.
Widely known to the public; familiar.
definitions.example: a household word; a household name

definitions.definition: A male duck.
A piece of hand luggage such as a suitcase or travelling bag.
To exert weight or force against, to act upon with force or weight; to exert pressure upon.
To activate a button or key by exerting a downward or forward force on it, and then releasing it.
To compress, squeeze.
definitions.example: to press fruit for the purpose of extracting the juice

definitions.definition: To clasp, hold in an embrace.
To reduce to a particular shape or form by pressure, especially flatten or smooth.
definitions.example: to press a hat

definitions.definition: To flatten a selected area of fabric using an iron with an up-and-down, not sliding, motion, so as to avoid disturbing adjacent areas.
To drive or thrust by pressure, to force in a certain direction.
definitions.example: to press a crowd back

definitions.definition: To weigh upon, oppress, trouble.
To force to a certain end or result; to urge strongly.
To try to force (something upon someone).
definitions.example: to press the Bible on an audience

definitions.definition: To hasten, urge onward.
definitions.example: to press a horse in a race

definitions.definition: To urge, beseech, entreat.
To lay stress upon.
To throng, crowd.
To print.
To force into service, particularly into naval service.
Under strain or deprivation
definitions.example: He found himself hard pressed.

definitions.definition: Recruited by a press gang

To manipulate objects, such as balls, clubs, beanbags, rings, etc. in an artful or artistic manner. Juggling may also include assorted other circus skills such as the diabolo, devil sticks, hat, and cigar box manipulation as well.
definitions.example: She can juggle flaming torches.

definitions.definition: To handle or manage many tasks at once.
definitions.example: He juggled home, school, and work for two years.

definitions.definition: To deceive by trick or artifice.
To joke or jest.
To perform magic tricks.
Producing harm in a stealthy, often gradual, manner.
Intending to entrap; alluring but harmful.
definitions.example: Hansel and Gretel were lured by the witch’s insidious gingerbread house.

definitions.definition: Treacherous.
definitions.example: The battle was lost due to the actions of insidious defectors.

definitions.definition: The act or process of infecting.
An uncontrolled growth of harmful microorganisms in a host.
A slope.
definitions.example: To reach the building, we had to climb a steep incline.

definitions.definition: To bend or move (something) out of a given plane or direction, often the horizontal or vertical.
definitions.example: He had to incline his body against the gusts to avoid being blown down in the storm.

definitions.definition: To slope.
definitions.example: Over the centuries the wind made the walls of the farmhouse incline.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the passive) To tend to do or believe something, or move or be moved in a certain direction, away from a point of view, attitude, etc.
definitions.example: He inclines to believe anything he reads in the newspapers.

definitions.definition: The plant Crocus sativus, a crocus.
A spice (seasoning) and colouring agent made from the stigma and part of the style of the plant, sometimes or formerly also used as a dye and insect repellent.
An orange-yellow colour, the colour of a lion's pelt.
To add saffron to (a food), for taste, colour etc.
definitions.example: saffroned water, saffroned rice.

definitions.definition: To give a saffron colour to (something).
To embellish.
Having an orange-yellow colour.
definitions.example: The saffron robe of a Buddhist monk.

definitions.definition: A dart or throwing blade, sometimes with multiple points, used as a weapon by ninja (or samurai).
To form cracks.
definitions.example: It's been so dry, the ground is starting to crack.

definitions.definition: To break apart under pressure.
definitions.example: When I tried to stand on the chair, it cracked.

definitions.definition: To become debilitated by psychological pressure.
definitions.example: Anyone would crack after being hounded like that.

definitions.definition: To break down or yield, especially under interrogation or torture.
definitions.example: When we showed him the pictures of the murder scene, he cracked.

definitions.definition: To make a cracking sound.
definitions.example: The bat cracked with authority and the ball went for six.

definitions.definition: (of a voice) To change rapidly in register.
definitions.example: His voice cracked with emotion.

definitions.definition: (of a pubescent boy's voice) To alternate between high and low register in the process of eventually lowering.
definitions.example: His voice finally cracked when he was fourteen.

definitions.definition: To make a sharply humorous comment.
definitions.example: "I would too, with a face like that," she cracked.

definitions.definition: To make a crack or cracks in.
definitions.example: The ball cracked the window.

definitions.definition: To break open or crush to small pieces by impact or stress.
definitions.example: You'll need a hammer to crack a black walnut.

definitions.definition: To strike forcefully.
definitions.example: She cracked him over the head with her handbag.

definitions.definition: To open slightly.
definitions.example: Could you please crack the window?

definitions.definition: To cause to yield under interrogation or other pressure. (Figurative)
definitions.example: They managed to crack him on the third day.

definitions.definition: To solve a difficult problem. (Figurative, from cracking a nut.)
definitions.example: I've finally cracked it, and of course the answer is obvious in hindsight.

definitions.definition: To overcome a security system or a component.
definitions.example: It took a minute to crack the lock, three minutes to crack the security system, and about twenty minutes to crack the safe.

definitions.definition: To cause to make a sharp sound.
definitions.example: to crack a whip

definitions.definition: To tell (a joke).
definitions.example: The performance was fine until he cracked that dead baby joke.

definitions.definition: To break down (a complex molecule), especially with the application of heat: to pyrolyse.
definitions.example: Acetone is cracked to ketene and methane at 700°C.

definitions.definition: To circumvent software restrictions such as regional coding or time limits.
definitions.example: That software licence will expire tomorrow unless we can crack it.

definitions.definition: To open a canned beverage, or any packaged drink or food.
definitions.example: I'd love to crack open a beer.

definitions.definition: To brag, boast.
To be ruined or impaired; to fail.
The thermal decomposition of a substance, especially that of crude petroleum in order to produce petrol / gasoline.
The formation of cracks on a surface.
The production of a crack sound.
definitions.example: the crackings of whips

definitions.definition: Great.
definitions.example: The race started at a cracking pace.

definitions.definition: Enjoyable.
definitions.example: We had a cracking time.

definitions.definition: Very, usually associated with praise.
definitions.example: It was a cracking good show.

definitions.definition: To enhance.
To make (someone or something) rich or richer.
definitions.example: Hobbies enrich lives.

definitions.definition: To adorn, ornate more richly.
To add nutrients or fertilizer to the soil; to fertilize.
To increase the amount of one isotope in a mixture of isotopes, especially in a nuclear fuel.
To add nutrients to foodstuffs; to fortify
To make to rise the proportion of a given constituent.
To add new elements, to complete.
An alcoholic liquor distilled from fermented grain and usually aged in oak barrels.
A drink of this liquor.
A light gig or carriage.
Talkative; chatty.
(usually with up) To amplify.
definitions.example: He asked the disk jockey to amp it up.

definitions.definition: (usually with up) To excite.
(usually with up) To intensify or increase.
definitions.example: 2014, Andy Paul, Amp Up Your Sales, AMACOM Books

definitions.definition: The act of arranging.
The manner of being arranged.
A collection of things that have been arranged.
A particular way in which items are organized.
(in the plural) Preparations for some undertaking.
An agreement.
An adaptation of a piece of music for other instruments, or in another style.
To influence or alter.
definitions.example: The experience affected me deeply.

definitions.definition: To move to emotion.
definitions.example: He was deeply affected by the tragic ending of the play.

definitions.definition: Of an illness or condition, to infect or harm (a part of the body).
definitions.example: Hepatitis affects the liver.

definitions.definition: To dispose or incline.
To tend to by affinity or disposition.
To assign; to appoint.
To make a show of; to put on a pretense of; to feign; to assume. To make a false display of.
definitions.example: He managed to affect a smile despite feeling quite miserable.

definitions.definition: To aim for, to try to obtain.
To feel affection for (someone); to like, be fond of.
To show a fondness for (something); to choose.
One's mood or inclination; mental state.
A desire, an appetite.
A subjective feeling experienced in response to a thought or other stimulus; mood, emotion, especially as demonstrated in external physical signs.
A flower, especially one indicating that a fruit tree is fruiting; a mass of such flowers.
definitions.example: The blossom has come early this year.

definitions.definition: The state or season of producing such flowers.
definitions.example: The orchard is in blossom.

definitions.definition: A blooming period or stage of development; something lovely that gives rich promise.
The colour of a horse that has white hairs intermixed with sorrel and bay hairs.
To have, or open into, blossoms; to bloom.
To begin to thrive or flourish.
To provide assistance to (someone or something).
definitions.example: He helped his grandfather cook breakfast.

definitions.definition: To assist (a person) in getting something, especially food or drink at table; used with to.
definitions.example: Help yourself to whatever's in the fridge.

definitions.definition: To contribute in some way to.
definitions.example: If you want to get a job, it helps to have some prior experience.

definitions.definition: To provide assistance.
definitions.example: Please, help!

definitions.definition: To avoid; to prevent; to refrain from; to restrain (oneself). Usually used in nonassertive contexts with can.
definitions.example: Are they going to beat us? Not if I can help it!

definitions.definition: The sound of an engine revving up.
To move with great speed; to zoom.
The sound of an engine revving up.
definitions.example: I never saw my uncle’s Ferrari, but I could always hear it going vroom as it flew past by my house.

definitions.definition: A shrine dedicated to some prophetic deity.
A person such as a priest through whom the deity is supposed to respond with prophecy or advice.
A prophetic response, often enigmatic or allegorical, so given.
A person considered to be a source of wisdom.
definitions.example: a literary oracle

definitions.definition: A wise sentence or decision of great authority.
One who communicates a divine command; an angel; a prophet.
A theoretical entity capable of answering some collection of questions.
(Jewish antiquity) The sanctuary, or most holy place in the temple; also, the temple itself.
To utter oracles or prophecies.
To pack too much.
A cloud in outer space consisting of gas or dust (e.g. a cloud formed after a star explodes).
A white spot or slight opacity of the cornea.
A cloudy appearance in the urine
Able to be neglected, ignored or excluded from consideration; too small or unimportant to be of concern.
definitions.example: We found errors, but their effects were negligible.

definitions.definition: A pass entitling the holder to admission to a show, concert, etc.
A pass entitling the holder to board a train, a bus, a plane, or other means of transportation
A citation for a traffic violation.
A permit to operate a machine on a construction site.
A service request, used to track complaints or requests that an issue be handled. (Generally technical support related).
A list of candidates for an election, or a particular theme to a candidate's manifesto.
definitions.example: Joe has joined the party's ticket for the county elections.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem; something that is needed.
definitions.example: I saw my first bike as my ticket to freedom.

definitions.definition: A little note or notice.
A tradesman's bill or account (hence the phrase on ticket and eventually on tick).
A label affixed to goods to show their price or description.
A certificate or token of a share in a lottery or other scheme for distributing money, goods, etc.
A visiting card.
To issue someone a ticket, as for travel or for a violation of a local or traffic law.
To mark with a ticket.
definitions.example: to ticket goods in a retail store

definitions.definition: The striking of one body against another; collision.
The force or energy of a collision of two objects.
definitions.example: The hatchet cut the wood on impact.

definitions.definition: A forced impinging.
definitions.example: His spine had an impingement; L4 and L5 made impact, which caused numbness in his leg.

definitions.definition: A significant or strong influence; an effect.
definitions.example: His friend's opinion had an impact on his decision.

definitions.definition: To collide or strike, the act of impinging.
definitions.example: When the hammer impacts the nail, it bends.

definitions.definition: To compress; to compact; to press into something or pack together.
definitions.example: The footprints of birds do not impact the soil in the way those of dinosaurs do.

definitions.definition: To influence; to affect; to have an impact on.
definitions.example: I can make the changes, but it will impact the schedule.

definitions.definition: To stamp or impress onto something.
definitions.example: Ideas impacted on the mind.

definitions.definition: A physical divider intended to block an area from view, or provide shelter from something dangerous.
definitions.example: a fire screen

definitions.definition: A material woven from fine wires intended to block animals or large particles from passing while allowing gasses, liquids and finer particles to pass.
(by analogy) Searching through a sample for a target; an act of screening

definitions.definition: Various forms or formats of information display
Definitions related to standing in the path of an opposing player
An erection of white canvas or wood placed on the boundary opposite a batsman to make the ball more easily visible.
A collection of less-valuable vessels that travel with a more valuable one for the latter's protection.
A dwarf wall or partition carried up to a certain height for separation and protection, as in a church, to separate the aisle from the choir, etc.
A large scarf.
To filter by passing through a screen.
definitions.example: Mary screened the beans to remove the clumps of gravel.

definitions.definition: To shelter or conceal.
To remove information, or censor intellectual material from viewing.
definitions.example: The news report was screened because it accused the politician of wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: To present publicly (on the screen).
definitions.example: The news report will be screened at 11:00 tonight.

definitions.definition: To fit with a screen.
definitions.example: We need to screen this porch. These bugs are driving me crazy.

definitions.definition: To examine patients or treat a sample in order to detect a chemical or a disease, or to assess susceptibility to a disease.
To search chemical libraries by means of a computational technique in order to identify chemical compounds which would potentially bind to a given biological target such as a protein.
To stand so as to block a defender from reaching a teammate.
To determine the source or subject matter of a call before deciding whether to answer the phone.
Someone who has accumulated a vast amount of fame; a high-level celebrity.
A general confusion or muddle.
A tossing or rolling about.
To roll around; to wallow.
To revel, luxuriate.
(of waves, billows) To rise and fall, to tumble over, to roll.
Any aquatic plant.
Relating to water; living in or near water, taking place in water.
A purplish mark on the skin due to leakage of blood from capillaries under the surface that have been damaged by a blow.
A dark mark on fruit or vegetables caused by a blow to the surface.
To strike (a person), originally with something flat or heavy, but now specifically in such a way as to discolour the skin without breaking it.
To damage the skin of (fruit or vegetables), in an analogous way.
Of fruit or vegetables, to gain bruises through being handled roughly.

definitions.definition: To become bruised.
definitions.example: I bruise easily.

definitions.definition: To fight with the fists; to box.
Someone who visits someone else; someone staying as a guest.
Someone who pays a visit to a specific place or event; a sightseer or tourist.
(usually in the plural) Someone, or a team, that is playing away from home.
A person authorized to visit an institution to see that it is being managed properly.
An extraterrestrial being on Earth for any reason.
An object which lands or passes by Earth or its orbit.
A head or overseer of an institution such as a college (in which case, equivalent to the university's chancellor) or cathedral or hospital, who resolves disputes, gives ceremonial speeches, etc.
The object in the visitor pattern that performs an operation on the elements of a structure one by one.
To shape metal by striking it, especially with a peen.
(often followed by a preposition such as "to" or "on") To fasten or attach (something) with a pin.
(usually in the passive) To cause (a piece) to be in a pin.
To pin down (someone).
To enclose; to confine; to pen; to pound.
To attach (an icon, application, etc.) to another item.

definitions.definition: To fix (an array in memory, a security certificate, etc.) so that it cannot be modified.
definitions.example: When marshaling data, the interop marshaler can copy or pin the data being marshaled.

definitions.definition: To cause an analog gauge to reach the stop pin at the high end of the range.
Having (a specified kind or number of) pins.

definitions.definition: The area in the interior of a country with a generally higher elevation; often hilly, but not generally mountainous (compare highlands).
The country, as against the town.
Of, relating to, or situated in the uplands.
To maintain in a juvenile state.
An article of clothing that covers the part of the body between the waist and the ankles or knees, and is divided into a separate part for each leg.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to magic.
Enchanting.
definitions.example: The fireworks created a magical atmosphere in that beautiful summer night.

definitions.definition: A dressing made from vegetable oil, raw egg yolks and seasoning, used on salads, with french fries, in sandwiches etc.
Any cold dish with that dressing as an ingredient.

definitions.definition: Any cream, for example for moisturizing the face or conditioning the hair, for which the base is egg yolks and oil.

definitions.definition: An eye lesion.
A displeasing sight; something prominently ugly or unsightly.

definitions.definition: To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted or familiar with; to have encountered.

definitions.definition: To experience.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).

definitions.definition: Any fact or situation which is known or familiar.

Identified as a specific type; famous, renowned.

definitions.definition: Accepted, familiar, researched.

To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
The act of appearing or coming into sight; the act of becoming visible to the eye.

definitions.definition: A thing seen; a phenomenon; an apparition.

definitions.definition: The way something looks; personal presence
Apparent likeness; the way which something or someone appears to others.

definitions.definition: That which is not substance, essence, hypostasis; the outward reality as opposed to the underlying reality

definitions.definition: The act of appearing in a particular place, or in society, a company, or any proceedings; a coming before the public in a particular character.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to measurement by volume.
That part of a cargo that produces revenue.

Sufficient accuracy to make the compressed result perceptually indistinguishable from the uncompressed input.

A pipe through which anything is drawn.

A small piece of leather, usually round, having a string attached to the center, which, when saturated with water and pressed upon a stone or other body having a smooth surface, adheres, by reason of the atmospheric pressure, with such force as to enable a considerable weight to be thus lifted by the string; formerly used by children as a plaything.
A suction cup.
An animal such as the octopus and remora, which adhere to other bodies with such organs.

A lollipop; a piece of candy which is sucked.
A hard drinker.

A person who is easily deceived, tricked or persuaded to do something; a naive person.

A period of 100 consecutive years; often specifically a numbered period with conventional start and end dates, e.g., the twentieth century, which stretches from (strictly) 1901 through 2000, or (informally) 1900 through 1999. The first century AD was from 1 to 100.

A unit in ancient Roman army, originally of 100 army soldiers as part of a cohort, later of more varied sizes (but typically containing 60 to 70 or 80) soldiers or other men (guards, police, firemen), commanded by a centurion.

A hundred things of the same kind; a hundred.
